ideas.title: Orbit Snap
ideas.one_liner: Tap to snap planets into orbit rings before they crash into your star
ideas.elevator_pitch: Rogue planets fly toward your star from all directions. Tap them at the exact moment they cross an orbit ring to lock them in. Each orbiting planet speeds up the ring, making future catches harder. Miss a tap and the planet smashes your star — three hits and you're dead.
ideas.core_verb_test: Tapping at the perfect moment to 'snap' a fast-moving object into a circular path is deeply satisfying — you feel the transition from chaos (linear trajectory) to order (smooth orbit). The snap has a magnetic quality, like clicking a puzzle piece into place at speed.
ideas.ten_second_test: A glowing star pulses at center screen. Two faint orbit rings surround it. A red planet streaks in from the top-right. You tap it as it crosses the first ring — SNAP — it locks into orbit with a satisfying click and begins circling. Immediately a second planet comes from the left, faster. You tap — locked. Now both planets orbit, the ring glows brighter, and a third planet appears. You're already in the flow.
ideas.reference_game: Most similar to Flappy Bird's timing precision, but with 360-degree spatial awareness like Fruit Ninja.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Every 10 captures adds a new orbit ring. Planet speed increases 5% per stage. Special planets appear: comets, black holes, binary planets.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-3: single ring, slow planets. Stage 4-7: two rings, medium speed. Stage 8+: three rings, fast planets, special types.
ideas.visual_style: Deep space black background. Neon-glow orbit rings. Planets as colored circles with motion trails.
ideas.sound_concept: Each snap plays a musical note based on the ring. Orbiting planets create ambient hum.
viral_hooks: Mesmerizing orbit patterns for video content

retention_system.meta_progression: Unlock new star types at score milestones.
retention_system.daily_hook: Daily challenge with specific handicap and daily leaderboard.

ideas.creator: spark

ideas.title: Ricochet Bloom
ideas.one_liner: Flick a ball and watch it bloom into a chain of perfect ricochets that paint the screen
ideas.elevator_pitch: Flick a ball into a field of bumpers. Each bounce splits the ball into two, creating an exponential ricochet cascade. Score based on bumpers hit before all balls stop. Bumpers decay — hit same one twice and it shatters. New bumpers grow where balls bloom.
ideas.core_mechanic: Single flick triggering exponential ricochet chains
ideas.core_verb_test: The flick itself is satisfying (drag-release with speed feedback), but the REAL satisfaction is watching your single action cascade into dozens of bounces — bowling-strike dopamine.
ideas.ten_second_test: Screen shows hexagonal bumpers. You flick a ball upward. It hits a bumper — splits into two. Each hits another — now four balls. Screen fills with bouncing, splitting balls painting color trails. Combo counter rockets: 4, 8, 16, 32.
ideas.reference_game: Most similar to Peggle's single-shot satisfaction combined with 2048's exponential growth.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Each stage new bumper layout. Special bumpers: magnets, prisms, voids.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-3: dense bumpers. Stage 4-7: sparser. Stage 8+: moving bumpers, gravity wells.
ideas.visual_style: White background painted by ball trails (watercolor bloom). Bumpers as pastel geometric shapes.
ideas.sound_concept: Ascending xylophone notes per bounce. Ball splits add harmony layers.
ideas.creator: spark

ideas.title: Gravity Flip Dash
ideas.one_liner: Tap to flip gravity while sprinting through a collapsing tunnel — fall up, fall down, never stop
ideas.elevator_pitch: A ball dashes forward through a procedural tunnel. Tap to flip gravity — fall up to ceiling, tap again to fall back. Obstacles from both sides. Tunnel collapses behind. Simple binary input, infinite depth.
ideas.core_verb_test: The gravity flip inverts your entire relationship with the screen. The momentum shift feels weighty with a brief slow-mo frame that makes each flip deliberate and powerful.
ideas.ten_second_test: Neon ball races rightward. Spike wall ahead on floor. TAP — gravity flips — ball arcs to ceiling. Ceiling gap appears. TAP — drops back. Tunnel crumbles behind. Speed increases. Narrow passage forces rapid flip-flip-flip.
ideas.reference_game: Most similar to Flappy Bird's binary input with VVVVVV's spatial inversion.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Speed +3% every 100m. New obstacles every 500m. Tunnel width narrows gradually.
ideas.difficulty_curve: 0-200m: gentle intro. 200-500m: both surface obstacles. 500-1000m: narrow, moving. 1000m+: pure reflex.
ideas.visual_style: Dark tunnel with neon grid lines (Tron aesthetic). Ball with bright glow trail.
ideas.sound_concept: Whoosh + bass note shift on flip. Electronic pulse matching speed.

retention_system.meta_progression: Unlock tunnel themes at distance milestones.
retention_system.daily_hook: Daily tunnel with global distance leaderboard.
retention_system.social_mechanic: Ghost runs — see friend's trail as you play.

ideas.creator: spark

ideas.title: Microwave Roulette
ideas.one_liner: Stop the microwave at the perfect second before your food explodes — but every item has a different sweet spot
ideas.elevator_pitch: Microwaving increasingly absurd items. Tap to stop timer in the green zone — too early and it's frozen, too late and it EXPLODES. Green zone shrinks every stage while distractions obscure your view.
ideas.core_mechanic: Precision tap timing on a spinning countdown
ideas.core_verb_test: Tapping to STOP something at the exact right moment triggers same dopamine as stopping a stopwatch on 0.00 — near-miss tension makes every tap electric.
ideas.ten_second_test: Microwave door slams shut. Pizza spins. Timer counts down. Green zone pulses. You watch... TAP! Perfect — pizza comes out with satisfying ding. Next item loads immediately.
ideas.reference_game: Most similar to Flappy Bird's timing tension with unique anticipation layer.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Green zone shrinks 3% per stage, timer speeds up, distractions increase. Boss items every 10 stages.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-5: generous zone (30%). Stage 6-15: medium (20%). Stage 16+: tiny (10%), heavy distractions.
ideas.visual_style: Chunky cartoon microwave. Colorful splatter explosions. Bold timer bar with pulsing green zone.
ideas.sound_concept: Microwave hum builds tension. DING on success. Comical explosion on failure.

retention_system.meta_progression: Unlock new kitchens with unique distractions.
retention_system.daily_hook: Daily mystery item with unique explosion animation.

ideas.creator: oddball

ideas.title: Toilet Unclogger
ideas.one_liner: Frantically plunge toilets before they overflow — each clog is weirder than the last
ideas.elevator_pitch: Toilets keep clogging with ridiculous objects. Tap rapidly to plunge, but each object has a different rhythm. Miss the rhythm and water rises. Overflow = game over. Multiple toilets at higher stages.
ideas.core_mechanic: Rhythmic rapid-tap with variable tempo matching
ideas.core_verb_test: Rapid tapping to plunge has same physicality as popping bubble wrap. Rhythm-matching adds skill layer where taps FEEL powerful on-beat.
ideas.ten_second_test: Toilet gurgles. Water rises. TAP TAP TAP! Plunger pushes down with squelch, water drops. Rubber duck pops out with squeak. Score +100! Next toilet immediately appears.
ideas.reference_game: Whack-a-Mole's frantic tapping + Guitar Hero's rhythm matching.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: New toilet added every 5 stages up to 3. Boss clogs every 8 stages. Rhythm complexity increases.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-5: single toilet, steady beat. Stage 6-10: two toilets, syncopated. Stage 11+: three toilets, mixed tempos.
ideas.visual_style: Cartoonish toilets with worried expressions. Oversized silly objects. Juicy splash animations.
ideas.sound_concept: Squelchy plunge sounds in rhythm. Comedic pop on dislodge. Flush fanfare on clear.
viral_hooks: 'You won't believe what was stuck' shareable moments

retention_system.meta_progression: Unlock bathroom venues with unique clog objects.

ideas.creator: oddball

ideas.title: Elevator Panic
ideas.one_liner: You're the elevator — drag people to the right floors before they rage-quit and the building collapses
ideas.elevator_pitch: People show what floor they want. Drag elevator up/down, tap to open doors. Passengers get angrier the longer they wait. Wrong floor doubles rage. Stack too many and cable snaps. Balance speed, accuracy, and capacity.
ideas.core_verb_test: Dragging feels like controlling a pulley — weight and momentum. The drag-and-release has slingshot satisfaction on vertical axis.
ideas.ten_second_test: Three cartoon people rush in showing floor numbers (3, 7, 1). You drag UP. Floor 3 — TAP to open. One hops out, coins fly. Drag UP to 7. Anger meters ticking. HURRY!
ideas.reference_game: Crazy Taxi's frantic delivery pressure with vertical drag mechanic.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Building gains 1 floor every 3 stages. Passenger spawn rate +10% per stage. New types every 5 stages.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-3: 5 floors, 1-2 passengers. Stage 4-8: 6-7 floors, 3-4 passengers. Stage 9+: 8-10 floors, VIPs and hazards.
ideas.visual_style: Side-cutaway view. Tiny expressive passengers. Cable visually strains. Quirky floor details.
ideas.sound_concept: Elevator ding on delivery. Cable creaking. Passengers mumble then yell. Ironic muzak throughout.

retention_system.meta_progression: Unlock building types with unique passengers and mechanics.
retention_system.daily_hook: Daily rush hour challenge with unique passenger types.
retention_system.social_mechanic: Highest floor and most passengers delivered leaderboards.

ideas.creator: oddball

ideas.title: Color Flood Panic
ideas.one_liner: Tap to paint zones before the rising flood of wrong colors drowns the board
ideas.elevator_pitch: A grid fills with random colors from the bottom up. Tap cells to paint them YOUR color. Chain same-color neighbors to clear chunks and push flood back. Wrong tap wastes time. Board shrinks and flood speeds up.
ideas.core_mechanic: Tap-to-paint territorial control under rising pressure
ideas.core_verb_test: Tapping to paint and watching a color ripple spread gives same dopamine as popping bubble wrap — each tap a tiny victory with immediate visual feedback.
ideas.ten_second_test: 6x6 grid. Bottom rows filling with colors rising upward. Tap a cell — turns yellow with ripple. Tap adjacent — both connect. Tap third — 3-chain clears with shatter, flood pushed down. '+3 COMBO'. Flood keeps rising.
ideas.reference_game: 2048's strategic planning meets Flood-It's color mechanics with real-time pressure.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Grid varies, flood faster each stage. New colors and locked cells added progressively.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1: slow flood, 3 colors. Stage 5: medium, 4 colors. Stage 10: fast, locked cells. Stage 20+: surge waves.
ideas.visual_style: Flat bold colors on dark background. Rounded squares with gradients. Wavy flood animation.
ideas.sound_concept: Bubbly pop on tap. Rising pitch for chains. Low rumble as flood approaches.

retention_system.meta_progression: Unlock color palettes every 10 stages. Permanent upgrades.

ideas.market_reasoning: Color-matching puzzles dominate casual charts but almost all are turn-based. Real-time color territory is wide-open gap.
ideas.creator: trendsetter

ideas.title: Rail Grind
ideas.one_liner: Hold to grind rails, release to jump — chain grinds across an endless skyline without falling
ideas.elevator_pitch: Skater grinding rails in the sky. Hold to grind, release to launch, tap to land on next rail. Miss timing and fall. Rails get shorter, gaps wider, rails start moving and disappearing. Longer chain = higher multiplier.
ideas.core_verb_test: Hold-and-release creates physical tension in thumb — you feel the grind building speed, then cathartic launch. Same tension-release as Angry Birds slingshot.
ideas.ten_second_test: Skater on rail with sparks. Holding screen — grinding forward. Rail end approaches. Release — launch upward with whoosh. Another rail ahead. Tap to land — PERFECT flash, x2 multiplier. Next rail shorter. Hold again.
ideas.reference_game: Flappy Bird's timing mastery + Tony Hawk's combo system.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Rails shorten progressively. Moving rails at stage 5. Disappearing at 10. Curved at 15.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-3: long rails, small gaps. Stage 4-7: medium, moving. Stage 8-12: short, disappearing. Stage 13+: all hazards.
ideas.visual_style: Neon cyberpunk skyline. Glowing rails. Silhouette skater with spark trail.
ideas.sound_concept: Metal grinding pitch-up with speed. Whoosh on launch. Clank on landing.

retention_system.meta_progression: Lifetime grinds unlock rail visual themes.
retention_system.daily_hook: Daily rail layout with fixed seed.
retention_system.social_mechanic: Ghost replays showing where friends fell.

ideas.market_reasoning: Endless runners saturated but endless GRINDERS don't exist. Hold-release mechanic underexplored. Skateboarding trending with Gen Z.
ideas.creator: trendsetter

ideas.title: Orbit Juggle
ideas.one_liner: Tap planets to keep them orbiting your star — let one drift off-screen and it's game over
ideas.elevator_pitch: Star at center. Planets orbit but decay. Tap to boost back into stable orbit. More planets spawn. Miss one = game over. Wrong timing knocks planets into each other. Managing 8+ planets with different speeds = frantic juggling.
ideas.core_mechanic: Tap-to-boost orbital management under increasing chaos
ideas.core_verb_test: Tapping a drifting planet and watching it snap back into orbit is the 'save!' feeling of catching a falling glass, repeated dozens of times per minute.
ideas.ten_second_test: Center star pulses. One planet orbits, trail turning green→yellow→red. Tap — orbit snaps tight with gravitational pulse, trail turns green. Second planet spawns. Third appears. Tap red one just in time — CLOSE CALL. Accidentally collide two planets — explosion. New one spawns.
ideas.reference_game: Plate-spinning tension of juggling games with Osmos's orbital physics aesthetic.
ideas.infinite_stage_design: Add planet every 10 seconds up to 12. Decay rate +10% per stage. Special planets and gravity wells introduced.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1: 2-3 slow. Stage 3: 4-5 mixed speeds. Stage 5: 6+ reverse orbit. Stage 8: gravity wells. Stage 10+: 8-12 with all hazards.
ideas.visual_style: Deep space with star field. Warm star glow. Colored planets with green→yellow→red trails.
ideas.sound_concept: Each planet has musical tone when tapped — emergent melody. Decay warning: crescendo hum.

retention_system.meta_progression: Unlock star types changing gameplay feel.
retention_system.daily_hook: Daily orbit challenge with survival time competition.
retention_system.social_mechanic: Share galaxy screenshot at peak chaos.

ideas.market_reasoning: Management/juggling games popular but space-orbit aesthetic underrepresented in hyper-casual. Emergent musicality adds unique viral angle.
ideas.creator: trendsetter
